places.description: One of the largest Buddhist stupas in the world and a UNESCO World Heritage Site. To make the most of Boudhanath Stupa, set aside about 2–3 hours and arrive when the light is soft—morning for calm, late afternoon for atmosphere and photos. Beyond the headline sights, slow down and notice the details: local craft shops, small courtyards, and everyday rituals that shape Kathmandu. If you’re visiting during October to April, crowds are usually manageable and the weather is comfortable. Getting there is straightforward via taxi or local bus from kathmandu, and you’ll find hotels and guesthouses nearby options nearby for any budget. Keep small cash for entry fees or snacks, dress respectfully at religious sites, and take time to try local foods and tea. A short guided walk can add context—history, symbolism, and etiquette—without turning the visit into something rushed.
places.transportation: Taxi or local bus from Kathmandu

World-famous trek leading to the base of Mount Everest. Everest Base Camp Trek is best approached as a journey rather than a checklist. Plan for roughly 12–14 days, including at least one or two buffer days for weather or acclimatization. Along the way you’ll move through small villages and teahouse stops where meals are simple but warming—dal bhat, noodles, soups—and mornings often start early to catch clear mountain views.  The route reaches high points around 5364 m, so pacing and hydration matter. Carry layered clothing for big temperature swings, and keep essentials in a daypack: water purification, snacks, a headlamp, and a basic first-aid kit. Transport typically involves flight to lukla, then trekking, and accommodation is usually teahouses. If you are new to altitude, a guide or trekking partner is a smart choice, and it’s wise to listen to your body more than your schedule.
safety_tips: Acclimatize properly, watch for altitude sickness
